criteria.name: Identifying Living Things
criteria.l4: Correctly identifies and explains why many different things are living (plants, animals, humans) with accurate reasons
criteria.l3: Identifies living things accurately and gives at least one reason why they are living
criteria.l2: Identifies some living things but may confuse some non-living items as living
criteria.l1: Cannot consistently distinguish living from non-living things

criteria.name: Basic Needs of Living Things
criteria.l4: Explains all basic needs (food, water, air, shelter) for plants and animals and why each is needed
criteria.l3: Names the basic needs of living things correctly
criteria.l2: Names some needs but incomplete or confuses needs of plants vs animals
criteria.l1: Cannot identify basic needs of living things

criteria.name: Observing Plant Parts
criteria.l4: Correctly names and describes functions of all major plant parts (roots, stems, leaves, flowers)
criteria.l3: Names most plant parts and their basic functions
criteria.l2: Names some plant parts but confuses their functions

criteria.name: Observing Animal Parts
criteria.l4: Identifies body parts of various animals and explains how they help the animal
criteria.l2: Identifies some animal parts but incomplete
criteria.l1: Cannot identify basic animal body parts

criteria.name: Growth and Changes in Living Things
criteria.l4: Describes how living things change over time and gives examples of growth stages
criteria.l3: Recognizes that living things grow and change
criteria.l2: Shows some understanding of growth but incomplete
criteria.l1: Does not understand that living things grow and change
